subject: PATIENT
heading: HOSPITAL COURSE:__page_no__2
text: hospital course: patient is a 65-year-old woman past medical history of essential hypertension and recurrent kidney stones. presented initially on [encounter_date] for septic shock and moderate right hydroureteronephrosis secondary to 10 mm distal ureter stone. initially septic with elevated lactic acids and admitted to icu. urology was consulted and patient underwent cystoscopy with right ureteral stent placement on [encounter_date]. treated empirically with ceftriaxone iv. found to have e. coli in her urine as well as e. coli bacteremia. clinically she improved on ceftriaxone. culture sensitivities showed pansensitive e. coli. urology cleared the patient for discharge with outpatient follow-up in 1 to 2 weeks. she will be discharged home in stable medical condition on ciprofloxacin for a total of 7 days of antibiotics. instructed to return to the hospital with any new or worsening symptoms. transitions of care critical issues: specialist follow-up: urology labs and procedures pending at discharge: no pending results. patient condition at discharge: stable

text: hospital course: patient is a 65-year-old woman past medical history of essential hypertension and recurrent kidney stones. presented initially on [encounter_date] for septic shock and moderate right hydroureteronephrosis secondary to 10 mm distal ureter stone. initially septic with elevated lactic acids and admitted to icu. urology was consulted and patient underwent cystoscopy with right ureteral stent placement on [encounter_date]. treated empirically with ceftriaxone iv. found to have e. coli in her urine as well as e. coli bacteremia. clinically she improved on ceftriaxone. culture sensitivities showed pansensitive e. coli. urology cleared the patient for discharge with outpatient follow-up in 1 to 2 weeks. she will be discharged home in stable medical condition on ciprofloxacin for a total of 7 days of antibiotics. instructed to return to the hospital with any new or worsening symptoms. transitions of care critical issues: specialist follow-up: urology labs and procedures pending at discharge: no pending results. patient condition at discharge: stable

subject: PATIENT
heading: History of Present Illness :: Interim History:__page_no__16
text: history of present illness interim history: still recovering from recent hospital visit for kidney stone and sepsis is still haivng some fatigue no other problems or concerns no fever or chills no pain j 11 no fever or chills no pain eating and drinking well discharge summary reviewed. transition of care inpatient name of the hospital [facility_name], transition from inpatient hospitalization, transition to home (own), admission date 02/24/2024, 30 day readmission no, number of in patient admissions in last 12 months. o, discharge date 02/28/2024, discharge summary report received yes operations during hospitalization: cystoscopy with r ureteral stent placement 2/25 procedures during hospitalization: echocardiogram hospital course: patient is a 65-year-old woman past medical history of essential hypertension and recurrent kidney stones. presented initially on 2/24 for septic shock and moderate right hydroureteronephrosis secondary to 10 mm distal ureter stone. initially septic with elevated lactic acids and admitted to icu. urology was consulted and patient underwent cystoscopy with right ureteral stent placement on 2/25. treated empirically with ceftriaxone iv. found to have e. coli in her urine as well as e. coli bacteremia. clinically she improved on ceftriaxone. culture sensitivities showed pansensitive e. coli. urology cleared the patient for discharge with outpatient follow-up in 1 to 2 weeks. she will be discharged home in stable medical condition on ciprofloxacin for a total of 7 days of antibiotics. instructed to return to the hospital with any new or worsening symptoms. transitions of care critical issues: specialist follow-up: urology labs and procedures pending at discharge: no pending results., date discharge summary was received: 02/28/2024, final discharge diagnoses received yes

text: history of present illness interim history: still recovering from recent hospital visit for kidney stone and sepsis is still haivng some fatigue no other problems or concerns no fever or chills no pain j 11 no fever or chills no pain eating and drinking well discharge summary reviewed. transition of care inpatient name of the hospital [facility_name], transition from inpatient hospitalization, transition to home (own), admission date 02/24/2024, 30 day readmission no, number of in patient admissions in last 12 months. o, discharge date 02/28/2024, discharge summary report received yes operations during hospitalization: cystoscopy with r ureteral stent placement 2/25 procedures during hospitalization: echocardiogram hospital course: patient is a 65-year-old woman past medical history of essential hypertension and recurrent kidney stones. presented initially on 2/24 for septic shock and moderate right hydroureteronephrosis secondary to 10 mm distal ureter stone. initially septic with elevated lactic acids and admitted to icu. urology was consulted and patient underwent cystoscopy with right ureteral stent placement on 2/25. treated empirically with ceftriaxone iv. found to have e. coli in her urine as well as e. coli bacteremia. clinically she improved on ceftriaxone. culture sensitivities showed pansensitive e. coli. urology cleared the patient for discharge with outpatient follow-up in 1 to 2 weeks. she will be discharged home in stable medical condition on ciprofloxacin for a total of 7 days of antibiotics. instructed to return to the hospital with any new or worsening symptoms. transitions of care critical issues: specialist follow-up: urology labs and procedures pending at discharge: no pending results., date discharge summary was received: 02/28/2024, final discharge diagnoses received yes
